LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PLANKTOS, INC., D2FUSION, INC., SOLAR ENERGY, LTD. AND NELSON SKALBANIA'S MOTION TO TRANSFER TO SAN MATEO COUNTY AND FOR SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THE SIGNED DECLARATION CONFORMS TO THE RULES OF CIVIL PROCEDURES AND IS ADMITTED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS NUMBERED 1, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 15, 18 AND 19 ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS NUMBERED 3, 10, 13 AND 16 ARE SUSTAINED. NUMBERS 6 AND 17, THE FIRST SENTENCE IS OVERRULED, THE SECOND SENTENCE IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT HAS 20 DAYS TO RESPOND. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PLANKTOS INC. D2FUSION INC, SOLAR ENERGY LTD, AND NELSON SKALBANIA DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER OF SKALBANIA TO BOTH THE SEXUAL HARASSMENT AND INTENTIONALLY CAUSING INJURY CLAIMS ARE SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE, IF SHE IS ABLE, SUFFICIENT FACTS SUPPORTING THOSE CLAIMS. THE DEMURRER OF THE ENTITY DEFENDANTS IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE, IF SHE IS ABLE, SPECIFIC FACTS STATING A FRAUD CLAIM AND OVERRULED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND INTENTIONALLY CAUSING INJURY. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PLANKTOS INC. D2FUSION INC, SOLAR ENERGY LTD, and NELSON SKALBANIA DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer of Skalbania to both the sexual harassment and intentionally causing injury claims are sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity to allege, if she is able, sufficient facts supporting those claims. The demurrer of the entity defendants is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the cause of action for fraud to give plaintiff the opportunity to allege, if she is able, specific facts stating a fraud claim and overruled as to the claims for breach of contract and intentionally causing injury. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/HEK)Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797

Off Calendar May-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
COURT CALL APPEARANCES: David Secrest, Esq., 650-726-7461 for defendants and moving parties PLANKTOS, INC., D2FUSION, INC., SOLAR ENERGY, LTD. AND NELSON SKALBANIA.
Philip Horne, Esq., 415-577-1170 for plaintiff and opposing party Mary Ruth Ladd

Ruling:
ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS PLANKTOS, INC., D2FUSION, INC., SOLAR ENERGY, LTD. AND NELSON SKALBANIA'S Motion To Transfer To San Mateo County And For Sanctions IS DENIED. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THE SIGNED DECLARATION CONFORMS TO THE RULES OF CIVIL PROCEDURES AND IS ADMITTED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS NUMBERED 1, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 15, 18 AND 19 ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS NUMBERED 3, 10, 13 AND 16 ARE SUSTAINED. NUMBERS 6 AND 17, THE FIRST SENTENCE IS OVERRULED, THE SECOND SENTENCE IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT HAS 20 DAYS TO RESPOND. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY, REPORTER MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600. (302/PJM/VC)
